title: Seg fault when importing CombineHarvester and some numpy versions

posts.name: Dominic William Stafford
posts.text:
Dear combine experts
Having installed combine and combine harvester in CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4 following the usual instructions, I got a segfault when doing:
import CombineHarvester.CombineTools.ch as ch
import numpy as np

in python. After some exploration of why this was affecting me but not others, I found the issue was I had numpy==1.23.5 installed in my user directory (confirmed this was the issue by installing this version in a venv on lxplus). After deleting my user numpy install I no longer got the error, so it’s not critical for me this gets fixed, but wanted to report it in case other people encounter the error (or if it’s easy to fix, which I doubt).
Best,
Dominic

After deleting my user numpy install I no longer got the error, so it’s not critical for me this gets fixed, but wanted to report it in case other people encounter the error (or if it’s easy to fix, which I doubt).

Best,

Dominic

posts.name: Sitian Qian
posts.text: I think I ran into similar issues with pandas etc. wonder if https://cms-sw.github.io/venv.html could be helpful for handling such issues.
posts.cooked_html: I think I ran into similar issues with pandas etc. wonder if https://cms-sw.github.io/venv.html could be helpful for handling such issues.

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi,
there are some incompatibilities with pandas and numpy, which could be inherited w/ local user installation. On top of the virtualenv  solution (which should be more stable), you could also try to set:
export PYTHONNOUSERSITE=nothingmeaningful

We may try as well to understand how much are the pandas library used, effectively.
-Andrea
